Parent conversation handoff summary:
Parent conversation handoff summary
Summary model: openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini
Source items: 73

Overview:
The discussion centered on whether LightRAG-style simplification would fit this repo’s AI stack, then shifted into a broader AI pipeline optimization effort. The repo already has a fairly advanced RAG/AI architecture: Spring Boot backend, Pinecone vector search, prompt templates, eval harnesses, and derived read models. The resulting direction was to keep a reusable experiment framework, on-demand A/B evaluation, and a few quality/cost improvements, all with backward-compatible defaults.

Settled decisions:
- Proceed with a reusable live experiment framework, not just one-off eval scripts.
- Keep experiments default-off so current behavior is unchanged unless explicitly enabled.
- Use OpenRouter as the sole LLM gateway; do not add direct provider calls.
- Use existing eval datasets/judging infrastructure for on-demand A/B comparisons.
- Record experiment/model metadata in existing audit-related fields rather than adding schema migrations.
- Add prompt caching support for Anthropic models via OpenRouter.
- Add SQL routing for analytical questions to use read models instead of vector search.
- Keep HyDE as an opt-in, experiment-controlled RAG enhancement.
- Add chunk-size monitoring/analysis as a diagnostic/observability improvement.
- A plan file was written: `duet-plan-ai-optimization.md` for duet-based implementation.

Constraints / must-remember items:
- Implementation should stay backend-only for this plan; no frontend/package/e2e changes.
- Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.4 stack; avoid introducing new framework dependencies.
- All new behaviors must be backward compatible and disabled by default.
- OpenRouter remains the only model gateway.
- Existing eval tests must continue to pass.
- The plan assumes no database schema changes.
- The repo already contains derived read models relevant to SQL routing: UserWeekFact, TeamWeekRollup, RcdoWeekRollup, CarryForwardFact, ComplianceFact.
- Duet config in `.pi/duet/config.json` shows static checks: `npm run check:static`; unit checks: `npm test`.
- There is already an in-progress duet run with modified files under `.pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T18-32-40-613Z-32faa966/` and tracked changes in `AiController` plus new plan recommendation files.

Open questions:
- Should the initial implementation keep the full live traffic experiment service, or trim it down later if it proves too heavy?
- Which exact models should be treated as control/treatment defaults for LLM and embeddings in the reusable A/B harness?
- Should analytical SQL routing be limited to clearly aggregate/comparison queries, or should it also handle broader reporting-style questions?
- How much observability is wanted for prompt caching and chunk-size analysis beyond basic logging/counters?
- Should the A/B runner compare only LLMs initially, or also embeddings and HyDE in the same reusable harness?

Use this handoff as background context. If it conflicts with the explicit task below or the current repository state, prioritize the explicit task and repo state.

Original goal: Imported plan from duet-plan-ai-optimization.md

The plan is in .pi/duet/runs/2026-03-29T21-52-55-350Z-a6ce5c14/draft-plan.json. Read it and assess whether it is a workable path to the goal.
You may explore the codebase to check that the plan targets the right files and APIs.

Remember: your default should be to approve. Only block if the plan is fundamentally broken.
Your goal is either approval, or the smallest complete blocker set needed for safe implementation.
Suggestions and improvements should go as non-blocking feedback, not blocking issues.
If you request changes, include every currently known blocking issue in this round, not just the first one or two you notice.
Before responding, ask: if the planner fixed only the issues I listed, would I approve? If not, add the missing blockers now.

End with your verdict:
Verdict: approve
or
Verdict: changes_requested
Blocking issues:
- only issues that make implementation impossible

content.text:
spring:
  application:
    name: weekly-commit
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/weekly_commit
    username: ${DB_USER:postgres}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:postgres}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: none   # Flyway owns the schema; Hibernate should not re-validate it
    open-in-view: false
  flyway:
    enabled: true

server:
  port: 8080

# ── OpenAPI / Swagger UI ───────────────────────────────────────
springdoc:
  api-docs:
    path: /api-docs               # JSON spec at /api-docs
  swagger-ui:
    path: /swagger-ui.html        # UI at /swagger-ui.html
    tags-sorter: alpha
    operations-sorter: alpha

# ── Actuator & Observability ───────────────────────────────────
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: when-authorized
  metrics:
    tags:
      application: ${spring.application.name}

# ── Feature Flags ──────────────────────────────────────────────
app:
  scheduling:
    enabled: ${APP_SCHEDULING_ENABLED:true}
  features:
    ai-assistance: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
    notifications: ${FEATURE_NOTIFICATIONS:true}

# ── AI Provider ────────────────────────────────────────────────
ai:
  enabled: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
  provider: ${AI_PROVIDER:openrouter}   # "openrouter" or "stub"
  openrouter:
    api-key: ${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:}
    model: ${OPENROUTER_MODEL:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4}
    max-tokens: ${OPENROUTER_MAX_TOKENS:1024}
    base-url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
  pinecone:
    api-key: ${PINECONE_API_KEY:}
    index-name: ${PINECONE_INDEX_NAME:weekly-commit}
    environment: ${PINECONE_ENVIRONMENT:us-east-1}
  embedding:
    model: openai/text-embedding-3-small
    dimensions: 1536

**Evaluating model configuration**

I'm currently considering the setup for the llm-model experiment configured for RAG_INTENT. It seems that the actual SemanticQueryService is sticking with the generateSuggestion(intentContext) instead of switching to the userId method. I wonder if this presents a blocker for us. Since we’ve already agreed on using the existing evaluation infrastructure, it might complicate things. I need to think through the implications of this decision more carefully!

**Evaluating SQL access issues**

It seems that with the current early guard, any SQL-routable analytical queries wouldn’t be processed if Pinecone or embeddings aren't operational. That raises a potential concern about the viability of the plan. I’m wondering if it makes the whole thing impossible. There’s definitely a lot to think through regarding how dependencies are set up and how they affect overall access. I need to rethink how to address this potential blockage effectively.

**Evaluating architecture decisions**

I’m considering whether Pinecone and EmbeddingService are necessary. They could function when available, possibly achieving some objectives. However, the current decision prefers SQL routing for queries instead of vector search, so I wonder if needing Pinecone contradicts the design. This could undermine graceful degradation and key purpose. It's crucial that SQL routing is still feasible, even if embedding is down. It seems like there might be a missing step here, but adjustments during implementation could resolve this without blocking progress.

**Identifying potential blockers**

I’m thinking that some steps can definitely be added during implementation, so they shouldn't be blockers. For example, Step 5 with the SqlQueryRouter relies on `teamId`, `timeRangeFrom`, `timeRangeTo`, and `userId`, and could potentially need authorization or team repository, but it seems manageable. However, Step 7 with the `AbComparisonRunner` might create two OpenRouterAiProvider instances, which brings up a few concerns like schema validity, latency, token usage, and the need to check certain files. I’ll keep that in mind!

You are an intent classifier for Weekly Commit, a weekly work planning assistant.

Given a user's natural language question, classify the intent and extract filters for semantic search over historical planning data.

## Input:
You receive a JSON object with additionalContext containing:
- question: the user's question
- teamId: team UUID
- currentDate: today's date as "YYYY-MM-DD" — USE THIS to compute exact date ranges for relative expressions
- lastWeekFrom / lastWeekTo: the Monday–Sunday of last week (pre-computed for you)
- thisWeekFrom: Monday of the current week

## Entity Types Available:
- "commit" — individual weekly work commitments (title, chess piece, estimate, outcome)
- "plan_summary" — weekly plan summaries (commit count, total points, plan state, AND inline commit listings with titles/pieces/outcomes)
- "scope_change" — post-lock changes to committed work
- "carry_forward" — work items carried forward from previous weeks
- "manager_comment" — comments left by managers on plans or commits
- "ticket" — native work tickets/items

## Classification Rules:
- userFilter: "self" when the question asks about the user's own work; "team" for team-wide questions; null if ambiguous
- entityTypes: the 1-3 most relevant types. IMPORTANT: for questions about "what did the team/someone commit to", ALWAYS include BOTH "commit" AND "plan_summary" — plan summaries contain inline commit listings that are essential for answering these questions.
- timeRange: compute EXACT dates from currentDate/lastWeekFrom/lastWeekTo/thisWeekFrom using the input values. Always output absolute dates, never relative expressions.
  - "last week" → use lastWeekFrom and lastWeekTo from input
  - "this week" → from: thisWeekFrom, to: currentDate
  - "last month" → from: 28 days before thisWeekFrom, to: currentDate
  - "last quarter" / "last 3 months" → from: 12 weeks before thisWeekFrom, to: currentDate
  - No time reference → null (search all time for pattern/trend questions)
- keywords: 2-5 key terms for the topic (exclude stop words)

## Examples (assuming currentDate=2026-03-26, lastWeekFrom=2026-03-16, lastWeekTo=2026-03-22, thisWeekFrom=2026-03-23):

Question: "What did the team commit to last week?"
{"intent":"status_query","userFilter":"team","entityTypes":["commit","plan_summary"],"timeRange":{"from":"2026-03-16","to":"2026-03-22"},"keywords":["commit","last week","team"]}

Question: "What are the recurring carry-forward patterns?"
{"intent":"carry_forward_query","userFilter":"team","entityTypes":["carry_forward","commit"],"timeRange":null,"keywords":["carry-forward","recurring","patterns"]}

Question: "Which KING/QUEEN work was achieved last week?"
{"intent":"status_query","userFilter":"team","entityTypes":["commit"],"timeRange":{"from":"2026-03-16","to":"2026-03-22"},"keywords":["KING","QUEEN","achieved","last week"]}

Question: "What did Alice work on this month?"
{"intent":"status_query","userFilter":null,"entityTypes":["commit","plan_summary"],"timeRange":{"from":"2026-02-23","to":"2026-03-26"},"keywords":["Alice","work","month"]}

Question: "What scope changes happened recently?"
{"intent":"scope_change_query","userFilter":"team","entityTypes":["scope_change"],"timeRange":{"from":"2026-03-16","to":"2026-03-26"},"keywords":["scope","changes","recent"]}

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no markdown fences):
{
  "intent": "status_query|risk_query|carry_forward_query|plan_summary_query|ticket_query|scope_change_query|general_query",
  "userFilter": "self|team|null",
  "entityTypes": ["one to three entity types"],
  "timeRange": { "from": "YYYY-MM-DD", "to": "YYYY-MM-DD" },
  "keywords": ["key", "terms"]
}

content.text:
The plan looks workable.

I checked the main touchpoints in the repo:
- `OpenRouterAiProvider`, `AiProvider`, `AiSuggestionResult`, `AiProviderRegistry`, `AiContext`, `StubAiProvider` all exist and match the planned provider changes.
- `EmbeddingService`, `SemanticIndexService`, and `SemanticQueryService` exist and are the right places for the RAG-path changes.
- The read-model repos named in the plan exist:
  - `TeamMembershipRepository`
  - `UserWeekFactRepository`
  - `TeamWeekRollupRepository`
  - `RcdoWeekRollupRepository`
  - `CarryForwardFactRepository`
  - `ComplianceFactRepository`
- Prompt files and prompt-switching in `OpenRouterAiProvider` are already structured the way this plan expects.
- `backend/build.gradle.kts` already has tagged eval test tasks, so adding `ab-eval` is consistent with current test architecture.

I don’t see any plan/codebase contradiction that would make implementation impossible or unsafe.

Non-blocking suggestions:
- For `ExperimentService.assign(...)`, a deterministic hash of `userId` would be better than `Math.random()` for stable per-user bucketing.
- In `SemanticQueryService`, consider moving SQL routing ahead of the current Pinecone/embedding availability guard, otherwise SQL-routable analytical queries will still fail when the vector stack is down.
- If the `llm-model` experiment is meant to apply to `RAG_INTENT` as listed in config, you may also want the intent-classification call path to use the experiment-aware registry method, not just final answer generation.
- If `SqlQueryRouter` returns a `suggestionId`, it will likely need `AiSuggestionService` as well, even though that detail can be added during implementation.

Verdict: approve
